text: I assembled a team of AIS each with
their own job and in this video I'm
going to put all their work together to
see if they can make a full game I
started by asking chat GPT to describe
the game it said a game where haunted
stuff happens in an office building I
thought this was pretty good because as
a YouTuber nothing scarier than a real
job I made a new project and had gbt
write me a script to let the player move
around copied it threw it in the game
and it doesn't work we're off to a great
start it rewrote it twice to get it to
work but once you could walk I wanted to
make the interior to the building so we
need a new AI I went to this website
called meshy which says it can create a
whole 3D model from just text so all I
have to do is prompt it to generate one
cubicle and what the hell am I looking
at okay how about just a desk it's a
little bit weird looking and you can
tell it's AI but it's good enough for me
after that I had it make a chair a
monitor a keyboard and a phone back in
the game I created walls in a floor to
represent what would eventually be the
office building I threw in the meshes
had just generated gave it some walls
and we have one cubicle after I copied
and pasted it a few times I wanted to
change the graphics of the building
because it was green and I'm pretty sure
most buildings don't do that this means
we need a new AI I used an image
generator and just slapped whatever gave
me onto the walls after a little trial
and error we got a boring carpet and
boring gray walls just like a real
office except right now it's one big
room and AI said the building has
multiple rooms so I started putting
putting up walls inside but then I
realized that was cheating one rule for
this project was that I wasn't going to
add anything myself I wanted to see what
the AI could really do so these walls
were against the rules so I deleted
these walls and the ones I had made
earlier and went back into meshy so it
could make me AI generated walls
honestly it was worse than what I
already had but I'm committed to not
letting humans do anything in this
project and instead of having it make me
a new floor I just
[Music]
[Applause]
we had a good start but the map wasn't
done so I kept generating models like
this chair that has two legs and only
one of them is touching the ground after
adding it all to the map we had three
rooms the first room with a bunch of
cubicles that make a maze the boss's
room which has a table for meetings and
the boss's desk and the storage room
which has a bunch of junk everywhere
Chachi PT also gave me some really
advanced settings to make the world a
little bit more scary I'm just kidding I
made it dark and added fog but this
helped with the environment it also told
me to add sticky notes that you can find
around the map so I made the model for
it in meshy and in the image generator I
made a yellow square that pops up on
your screen GPT wrote some messages that
you can find on these sticky notes like
this one that says Sarah from HR hasn't
been seen for days and her office is
empty but I heard her typing late last
night but the stuff in this note doesn't
really make any sense if there's no
enemy so let's add one I asked it to
describe an enemy and it told me about
the supervisor which is maybe the
coldest name I've ever heard it
described him as a creepy old white dude
dude I went back into Meshi and tried a
couple times to make this guy and I
ended up going with this dude who turned
out ugly as hell come on man I don't
think it did this on purpose but it
works for the game I put him in the game
and now we need to animate him to follow
the player normally I would just make it
myself or use a website like mixo but
that's not a low for this project so
it's time for the fourth AI called
anything World which is supposed to
animate our characters I uploaded this
supervisor to the website and in return
it gave me
this yeah some of the animations turned
out really weird but I liked this one
even though the legs were glued together
I thought it gave a really creepy feel
and I decided to just run with it with
the short script to have him follow you
around the game was scary
already but this isn't quite what we
want in the original idea GPT said he
patrols the office and makes sure you're
working to reflect this in the game I
had to write some code to detect if you
were at your cubicle if you were the
supervisor would leave you alone and he
would wander around the office but if
you leave he'll chase you around until
he catches you you can tell which one's
your desk because it has no chair and
it's the only one with a light so you
always know where to get back to I also
added another sticky note and had GPT
write a note to self which basically
just explains that you're safe at your
own desk with that working I wanted to
make him jump scare you but I really
wasn't sure how to do it normally I
would give the enemy a scary animation
but the AI didn't make any so I told
myself I would come up with something
and get back to this later in the
meantime we need to way to get to the
other rooms because the doors are locked
by default GPT told me to add a key to
get into the boss's room a code to get
into the storage room and a key card to
escape the building I started with the
key to get into the boss's room so I
generated it and threw it in the corner
the problem with this is that it was
hard to pick up because you couldn't
really see where you were looking the
most obvious solution is to just give
the player a Crosshair so I went back
into the image generator and asked it
for a couple but they turned out pretty
weird so I asked it for a blank white
square and used that instead and now we
have a Crosshair but I still didn't like
that the key was on the ground so I
generated filing cabinets and put it on
top of them now it was a lot easier to
see and pick up GPT also updated one of
the sticky notes to tell you where to
find this key with the key done I could
move on to the door but yet again I ran
into a problem here in order to get the
door to open you need to make an
animation which I'm not allowed to do
and the AI from earlier works with
pretty much anything but doors but there
was this plugin that lets you move
things using code GPT generated some
code for this and it worked just just as
good as an animation with that the main
room was done so I could move on to the
boss's room and this one ended up being
pretty difficult like we said earlier
the boss's room leads into the storage
room using a code and that's our problem
to make a code work you would normally
make some kind of pop up on the screen
where the player can click and enter
whatever they want but I wasn't allowed
to make this myself not to worry the
image generator can make
[Music]
it or so I thought yeah I forgot AI
can't write letters or numbers so I
would need to come up with something new
for this code I decided to use colors
thinking that these would generate
better honestly they were still kind of
a pain but I eventually got this one
which I felt like was good enough I
generated some code to have the buttons
pop up and detect the pattern of your
clicks and GPT also wrote text for a
sticky note which told you the code to
the door finally it was time for the
storage room here you can find the key
card to win the game so I threw it on
this shelf and if you bring it to the
exit you win in a black screen shows up
except this isn't allowed because I
added the black screen myself so we
would need something made by AI I went
back to the image generator and tried to
make a screen that says you win and even
though AI sucks with words I thought it
would be able to do this I was wrong
pretty stupid of me but I just kept
regenerating it over and over until I
got this one it did have two periods
which was weird but I felt like it was
the best we could get now the gameplay
Loop was almost done you could spawn in
find the key the code the key card and
Escape but one major thing is still
missing sound GPT had earlier said that
the supervisor murmurs while he walks
around so I had to write out some voice
lines like the Shadows know your fear I
can feel it but to actually generate the
voice I used the fifth AI 11 Labs it was
free so I give it a voice line but well
just listen the Shadows know your fear I
can feel it at subscribe subscribe
subscribe yeah they weren't scary at all
until I found this ASMR voice it seems
weird for a horror game but I thought it
could work I gave it one of the voice
lines and it was okay the Shadows know
your fear I can feel it but I still felt
like it could do better that's where the
6th AI comes in it's used to generate
sound effects but it also has a section
for voice effects I took the audio clip
and threw it in this AI to create a
subtle effect that was more like what I
was looking for the Shadows know your
fear I can feel it back in the game I
generated some code to have these
recordings play at random intervals with
this working only sound effects were
left but I ran into a really weird
problem here first of all the sounds
themselves were
[Music]
interesting but that's not the problem
the problem is that for this 2C sound
the audio file has like 4 seconds of
Silence at the beginning this means when
you play it in the game there is a huge
delay before you actually hear
anything normally you'd open it in an
audio editor and trim the intro but we
can't do that so I found a shiny new 7th
AI this one cut out the Silence of any
audio file so I ran all of the sound
effects through it and it got rid of the
Annoying
delay as I finished this up I remembered
there's still no jump scare earlier I
said I was just going to put it off for
later but now it's later so let's get to
work not having a scare animation like I
usually do was definitely a problem but
I felt like I could work around found it
right now if the enemy catches you it
plays this sound I generated and makes
you look at him but this on its own
was disappointing I looked at other jump
scares for inspiration to see how I
could make it scary without animation
after doing some intense research of
scrolling through jump scares on YouTube
I learned that pretty much all jump
scares have a loud sound camera shake
and a flashing light I just needed to
add these two and I would be golden GPT
wrote some code to add that and the jump
scare turned out pretty good I am
warning you though the flashing light is
pretty intense so this is an epilepsy
warning but finally the game was
finished the conversation with chat GPT
was so long that now it won't write me
any more code because it's too tired now
to answer the question is this a viable
strategy for making games in my opinion
kind of a big problem was that the
models are made in a way that's really
bad for performance and a lot of the
textures aren't quite right I think it's
a pretty solid way to get a prototype
working quickly but it wouldn't fly as a
paid game on Steam anyway if you like
this video check out this one where I
